protocol.name: xfce_output_private_v1
protocol.copyright:

interface.name: xfce_output_manager_private_v1
interface.description:
This protocol advertises extensions to the existing wl_output and xdg_output protocols.
It is intended to be private to the Xfce desktop implementation, and should not be used by other applications. This protocol may introduce incompatible changes at any time, without warning, and without following normal, accepted guidelines about Wayland protocol versioning.
This protocol depends on the xdg_output protocol.

event.name: primary_output

description.summary: Emitted when the primary output changes

event.description: The user or compositor may have designated an output as "primary", which is the output that might usually hold the desktop's main UI elements, like a panel or dock.

request.name: get_xfce_output
request.description: This creates a new xfce_output_v1 object for the given xdg_output.

arg.name: output
arg.summary: Output to request a xfce_output_v1 object for

event.name: serial

description.summary: Advertises the serial number of a device

event.description:
Emitted when the serial number (if available) of the device attached to the output has changed. In practice, this should only happen once after the xfce_output_v1 object has been created, since a device serial number should never change while it is connected.
Once all properties on this interface have been advertised, the compositor will send a wl_output done event, similar to how wl_output and xdg_output (as of version 3) work. So for example, if you've bound to the wl_output, xdg_output (at version 3 or higher), and xfce_output_v1, you should expect 3 wl_output done events any time a property on the output changes.

event.name: workarea_size

description.summary: Advertises the usable space on the output

event.description:
An output's workarea is the area that is usable for the display of regular surfaces. For example, a panel or dock may reserve space at the edge of the screen where other surfaces should not be placed, so the area taken up by the panel or dock would be excluded by the workkarea.
For most applications, merely knowing the size of the workarea is enough (for example, to calculate how many notifications can be shown in a single column on screen), and withholding coordinates avoids leaking information about the location of panels or docks.
Sizes are presented in logical coordinates that may be scaled (see xdg_output.logical_size for details).
Once all properties on this interface have been advertised, the compositor will send a wl_output done event, similar to how wl_output and xdg_output (as of version 3) work. So for example, if you've bound to the wl_output, xdg_output (at version 3 or higher), and xfce_output_v1, you should expect 3 wl_output done events any time a property on the output changes.

event.name: seat_enter

description.summary: Emitted when a seat enters this output

event.description:
It is up to the compositor to define exactly what this means, but in general it means that focus is on a surface on this output, whether keyboard or pointer focus. It could also mean that the seat's pointer has moved from one output to another, even if keyboard focus has not.
This "seat focus" can only be present on one output at a time; aside from the first ever instance of this event, the compositor will always send a seat_leave event before the next seat_enter.

event.name: seat_leave

description.summary: Emitted when a seat leaves this output

event.description:
It is up to the compositor to define exactly what this means, but in general it means that focus has left a surface on this output (and is about to move to a surface on another output), or simply that the seat's pointer position is no longer within this output.
This "seat focus" can only be present on one output at a time; aside from the first ever instance of this event, the compositor will always send a seat_leave event before the next seat_enter.
